# unifi_traffic_matching_list (Resource)

A traffic matching list: a named, reusable set of IPv4, IPv6, or port matchers that firewall policies can reference.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# A reusable IPv4 list (host, subnet, and range) that firewall policies can reference.
resource "unifi_traffic_matching_list" "blocked_sources" {
  name = "Blocked sources"
  type = "IPV4_ADDRESSES"

  items = [
    {
      match_type = "SUBNET"
      value      = "192.0.2.0/24"
    },
    {
      match_type = "IP_ADDRESS"
      value      = "198.51.100.10"
    },
    {
      match_type = "IP_ADDRESS_RANGE"
      start      = "203.0.113.10"
      stop       = "203.0.113.20"
    },
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `items` (Attributes List) Matchers in the list. Allowed match_type and fields depend on the list type. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--items))
- `name` (String) List name.
- `type` (String) List kind: IPV4_ADDRESSES, IPV6_ADDRESSES, or PORTS. Changing it replaces the list.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Traffic matching list UUID.

<a id="nestedatt--items"></a>
### Nested Schema for `items`

Required:

- `match_type` (String) Matcher variant. IPV4: IP_ADDRESS, IP_ADDRESS_RANGE, SUBNET. IPV6: IP_ADDRESS, SUBNET. PORTS: PORT_NUMBER, PORT_NUMBER_RANGE.

Optional:

- `start` (String) Range start, for IP_ADDRESS_RANGE / PORT_NUMBER_RANGE.
- `stop` (String) Range stop, for IP_ADDRESS_RANGE / PORT_NUMBER_RANGE.
- `value` (String) Single value: an address (IP_ADDRESS), a CIDR (SUBNET), or a port number (PORT_NUMBER).
